title: I'm a scientist. One conversation led me to rethink climate change

paragraphs.english: Jacob Martin believes that we need respectful debate in the climate change discussion. Source: SBS
paragraphs.pidgin: Jacob Martin believes dat we need respectful debate for di climate change discussion. Source: SBS

paragraphs.english: Insight asks students, families, politicians, protesters and academics whether we've lost the ability to disagree well. Watch Death of Debate on SBS or on SBS On Demand.
paragraphs.pidgin: Insight asks students, families, politicians, protesters and academics whether we've lose di ability to disagree well. Watch Death of Debate on SBS or on SBS On Demand.

paragraphs.english: Growing up in a rural community, there was a strong sense that city-based policymakers didn't understand the challenges of country life.
paragraphs.pidgin: Growing up for a rural community, e dey a strong sense dat city-based policymakers no understand di challenges of country life.

paragraphs.english: In my small New Zealand farming town, everything revolved around sport, church and the land.
paragraphs.pidgin: For my small New Zealand farming town, everytin revolved around sport, church and di land.

paragraphs.english: Fuel prices and environmental regulations were immediate concerns. I vividly remember a farmer driving his tractor up the steps of parliament to protest emissions taxes.
paragraphs.pidgin: Fuel prices and environmental regulations bin dey immediate concerns. I vividly remember a farmer driving im tractor up di steps of parliament to protest emissions taxes.

paragraphs.english: We didn't talk much about climate change, but if it did come up, it was a joke or in a dismissive context. If we had a cold snap, we would say, "We could use a bit of that global warming right now".
paragraphs.pidgin: We no talk plenti about climate change, but if e do come up, e bin na a joke or for a dismissive context. If we bin get a cold snap, we go tok, "We fit use a bit of dat global warming now now".

paragraphs.english: My views on this topic would later start to change — thanks to a debate.
paragraphs.pidgin: My views on dis topic go later start to change — thanks to a debate.

paragraphs.english: In high school, I had a teacher who was drawn to conspiracy theories. His classes were entertaining, and I felt encouraged to question widely held views.
paragraphs.pidgin: For high school, I bin get a teacher who bin drawn to conspiracy theories. Im classes bin dey entertaine, and I feel encouraged to question widely held views.

paragraphs.english: I had mentors and role models at home and church who helped me critically assess politically motivated conspiracies. But I felt there weren't many people who could help me with scientific topics.
paragraphs.pidgin: I bin get mentors and role models for house and church who help me critically assess politically motivated conspiracies. But I feel there no bin dey plenti pipul who fit help me wit scientific topics.

paragraphs.english: I had turned to the internet and old-school email forums, when I came across confident voices claiming climate change was a hoax.
paragraphs.pidgin: I bin $2 to di internet and old-school email forums, when I come across confident voices claiming climate change bin a hoax.

paragraphs.english: I wouldn't have called myself a climate change denier, but I felt strongly that the science was not settled.
paragraphs.pidgin: I wouldn't don call myself a climate change denier, but I feel strongly dat di science bin no settled.

paragraphs.english: Despite my doubts, I was a teenager who was excited about alternative energy sources.
paragraphs.pidgin: Despite my doubts, I bin a teenager who bin excited about alternative energy sources.

paragraphs.english: Burning fossil fuels felt like an expensive use of a limited resource. I was also concerned about energy security and the human health impacts of smog and soot.
paragraphs.pidgin: Burning fossil fuels feel like an expensive use of a limited resource. I bin also concerned about energy security and di human health impacts of smog and soot.

paragraphs.english: So, at 16 years old in 2007, I built a renewable energy project with help from a scientific mentor and the local welding workshop.
paragraphs.pidgin: So, for 16 years old for 2007, I build a renewable energy project wit help from a scientific mentor and di local welding workshop.

paragraphs.english: My project placed in the local science fair, and I went on to compete nationally.
paragraphs.pidgin: My project placed for di local science fair, and I go on to compete nationally.

paragraphs.english: As a national finalist, I was invited with others to a university where I toured the climate change research lab facilities. I was amazed to see where the ice cores were stored and the scientific instruments used to measure carbon dioxide in both the ice and atmosphere.
paragraphs.pidgin: As a national finalist, I bin invited wit others to a university where I toured di climate change research lab facilities. I bin amazed to see where di ice cores bin dey stored and di scientific instruments use to measure carbon dioxide for both di ice and atmosphere.

paragraphs.english: One climate scientist presented a talk on the science of climate change and I decided to challenge her.
paragraphs.pidgin: One climate scientist presented a talk on di science of climate change and I decide to wahala im.

paragraphs.english: I brought up the argument that carbon dioxide historically lags temperature — implying that carbon dioxide couldn't be the cause of global warming.
paragraphs.pidgin: I raise di argument dat carbon dioxide historically lags temperature — implying dat carbon dioxide couldn't be di cause of global warming.

paragraphs.english: She listened, did not dismiss me, and then addressed my argument.
paragraphs.pidgin: Im listened, no dismiss me, and then addressed my argument.

paragraphs.english: Yes, in natural cycles carbon dioxide used to follow temperature. But today, carbon dioxide is rising before temperature. That reversal is evidence of human influence.
paragraphs.pidgin: Yes, for natural cycles carbon dioxide use to follow temperature. But today, carbon dioxide dey rise before temperature. Dat reversal na evidence of human influence.

paragraphs.english: After the talk, she pointed me to the website of a not-for-profit organisation that answered common questions about climate science and explained counterarguments with peer-reviewed research.
paragraphs.pidgin: After di talk, im pointed me to di website of a no-for-profit organisation dat answered common questions about climate science and explained counterarguments wit peer-reviewed research.

paragraphs.english: It was one of the first times I had encountered a debate about climate science where I was invited to assess the evidence for myself — and better understand both the science and the scientists behind it.
paragraphs.pidgin: e bin na one of di first times I bin $2 a debate about climate science where I bin invited to assess di evidence for myself — and better understand both di science and di scientists behind e.

paragraphs.english: Learning the facts changed my mind, but it still took a few years for me to care about the impact of climate change.
paragraphs.pidgin: Learning di facts change my mind, but e still take a small small years for me to care about di impact of climate change.

paragraphs.english: At university, I was mentored by a cancer researcher and theologian Graeme Finlay who introduced me to the work of the late physicist Sir John Houghton, a founding member of the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change.
paragraphs.pidgin: For university, I bin mentored by a cancer researcher and theologian Graeme Finlay who introduced me to di work of di late physicist Sir John Houghton, a founding member of di Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change.

paragraphs.english: The work of these two scientists helped me see that there wasn’t a conflict between my faith and science — my faith supported my calling to science.
paragraphs.pidgin: Di work of dis ones two scientists help me see dat there wasn’t a wahala between my faith and science — my faith support my calling to science.
